title: magnolia champaca var. champaca
intro: Magnolia champaca var. champaca, also known as the yellow champaca or Michelia champaca, is a flowering tree native to Southeast Asia and parts of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the Magnoliaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like linalool and geraniol, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Magnolia champaca var. champaca include its use in treating anxiety, insomnia, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in traditional medicine to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to address various health concerns and promote overall well-being.
